channel.title: Nicotine Science and Policy
channel.description: Daily digest of views, debates and discussions on nicotine science, policy, regulation and advocacy.

item.title: FDA Authorizes 20 ZYN Nicotine Pouches to Be Marketed with Specific Modified Risk Claim
item.description: The FDA’s authorisation of modified risk claims for 20 ZYN nicotine pouch products marks an important step for evidence-based nicotine communication. Adults who smoke should be able to receive clear information that switching completely from cigarettes to authorised smoke-free products can reduce specific disease risks. The decision recognises that not all nicotine products carry the same harm, and that accurate relative-risk messaging can support better choices. Public health should make lower-risk information visible, not harder to say. (Published on July 01, 2026byfda.gov)

item.title: Prevalence and correlates of e-cigarette use among older adults aged 60 years and over
item.description: New evidence from Scotland shows that e-cigarette use among adults aged 60 and over is low, but strongly concentrated among current and former smokers. That matters because older adults carry a high burden of smoking-related disease and may need more tailored cessation support. The findings point to the need for balanced risk communication that helps older smokers understand the difference between combustible tobacco and lower-risk alternatives. Harm reduction should not leave older adults behind. (Published on July 01, 2026bynih.gov)
